reasoning: I am voting for Shantanu because the March gazette is real—it exists on paper, not like the four-year-old promises. But I will not lie to myself: seeing fourteen certificates given out five days before this election, when I have been waiting since December 2019, makes me feel like my half-torn certificate is a political prop. I vote for him because the CAA mechanism is still the only path forward for my father, but this vote is not hope anymore—it is desperation disguised as choice. If Shantanu gets another term as minister, maybe he will actually help. Mamata spread fear that CAA is a trap. The Left has never mattered to Matua people. So I vote BJP, but I vote angry.
primary_drivers: The CAA gazette is real and still the only path to my father's citizenship
Shantanu as Union Minister is the only politician with institutional power to help, despite betraying my timeline
TMC and Left offer no path to citizenship—only schemes that keep me fed while my document stays half-torn
